questions.question: What is the probability of flipping a fair coin three times and getting all heads?
What is the sum of probabilities of all possible mutually exclusive outcomes in a sample space?
In how many ways can 4 people be seated in a straight line?
What is the value of the combinatorial coefficient C(5, 2)?
How many unique permutations can be made from the letters in the word 'APP'?
How many distinct subsets can be formed from a set containing 5 elements?
If P is true and Q is false, what is the truth value of the implication P -> Q?
What is a logical statement called if it evaluates to true under all possible truth value assignments?
What is the negation of the statement 'All primes are odd'?
answers: There exists at least one prime that is not odd

questions.question: Which logical connective represents an 'exclusive or' (XOR)?
answers: At least one input is true
